cards.title: Runtime validates session metadata and clamps granted permissions before opening sessions
cards.summary: Before any runtime session is accepted, Redeven validates `session_meta`, rejects unsupported app/channel combinations, intersects the control-plane grant with the local permission policy, and then applies extra app-specific gates for Code App and Port Forward.
cards.mechanism: When `grant_server` arrives on the control channel, the runtime checks the channel id, endpoint id, and `floe_app`, resolves the local cap via `PermissionPolicy.ResolveCap`, intersects that cap with the declared read/write/execute grant, writes the clamped flags back into the session snapshot, and refuses Code App or Port Forward sessions that do not satisfy stricter runtime-side requirements.
cards.boundaries: Browser or UI-side permission claims remain non-authoritative. This card only holds while the runtime continues enforcing local caps plus per-app validation before `runDataSession` starts.
cards.invalid_conditions: This card becomes invalid if session startup begins trusting browser-side permission state, skips local cap intersection, or stops doing per-app validation before opening data sessions.

evidence.note: PermissionPolicy is documented as clamping control-plane session metadata to a user-approved local maximum.
ResolveCap intersects global, per-user, and per-app caps.
Unsupported floe_app values are rejected before runtime session startup.
Granted permissions are intersected with the resolved local cap.
Effective permissions overwrite the session metadata snapshot used by the runtime.
Code App sessions require a valid codespace id and full read/write/execute access.
Port Forward sessions require a valid forward id and execute capability.

cards.title: Runtime transport rides Flowersec sessions while terminal lifecycle rides Floeterm managers
cards.summary: Redeven runtime builds control and data sessions on Flowersec client and endpoint primitives, while terminal lifecycle inside the runtime is delegated to Floeterm's terminal-go manager.
cards.mechanism: Redeven pins released `flowersec-go` and `terminal-go` versions in `go.mod`. The agent connects the control channel through `fsclient.ConnectDirect`, opens data sessions through `endpoint.ConnectTunnel`, and wraps `termgo.NewManager` so terminal sessions, PTY activation, and lifecycle bookkeeping stay inside Floeterm's manager abstraction.
cards.boundaries: Compatibility depends on these transport and terminal interfaces staying aligned across released versions. Replacing or bypassing them can break control and data channel behavior, stream semantics, or terminal session lifecycle.
cards.invalid_conditions: This card becomes invalid if runtime transport no longer uses Flowersec client and endpoint session abstractions or terminal lifecycle moves off Floeterm manager semantics.

evidence.note: Redeven pins floeterm terminal-go in the runtime module.
Redeven pins flowersec-go in the runtime module.
Agent imports Flowersec client, endpoint, proxy, and RPC packages.
The control channel connects through fsclient.ConnectDirect.
Runtime data sessions connect through endpoint.ConnectTunnel.
Runtime terminal manager wraps floeterm terminal-go plus Flowersec RPC types.
Runtime instantiates termgo.NewManager with Redeven shell and logging config.
Floeterm exposes the terminal manager constructor Redeven embeds.
Floeterm manages dormant logical terminal sessions before PTY activation.
Flowersec client sessions expose RPC, stream opening, ping, and close semantics.
Flowersec endpoint sessions expose ServeStreams and OpenStream over the secure mux.

cards.title: Knowledge bundle source is curated, deterministic, and embedded into the runtime binary
cards.summary: Redeven builds its knowledge bundle from curated source files under `internal/knowledge/source`, verifies deterministic dist outputs, and embeds the resulting bundle into the binary used by runtime and AI features.
cards.mechanism: `build_assets.sh` invokes `build_knowledge_bundle.sh`, which runs `cmd/knowledge-bundle` against explicit source and dist roots. The builder loads the manifest, cards, and indices, hashes the full source tree into `source_sha256`, writes bundle plus manifest plus sha outputs, CI validates source integrity and stale dist, and `go:embed` makes the bundle available to runtime search code.
cards.boundaries: The bundle remains auditable only while source cards are the sole authoring surface and `internal/knowledge/dist/*` stays a generated verification artifact set rather than hand-edited truth.
cards.invalid_conditions: This card becomes invalid if build-time knowledge starts depending on ad-hoc model output, if checked-in dist diverges from source without verification failures, or if runtime stops using embedded bundle assets.

evidence.note: Embedded asset builds always include the knowledge bundle stage.
Bundle script resolves dedicated source and dist roots under internal/knowledge.
Bundle command always rebuilds from source before validate, verify, or write modes.
BuildFromSource assembles the bundle from manifest, cards, and indices.
Source tree hashing is recorded as source_sha256 provenance.
VerifyDistFiles rejects stale checked-in dist artifacts.
knowledge_bundle.json and its manifest are embedded into the Go binary.
Runtime search loads the embedded bundle lazily from embed FS.
Integrity checks require the source manifest and index files before validation.
CI runs source integrity plus dist verification on every main and PR check.

cards.title: Release workflow publishes knowledge verification assets alongside CLI and desktop artifacts
cards.summary: GitHub releases ship `knowledge_bundle.manifest.json` and `knowledge_bundle.sha256` together with CLI tarballs, desktop packages, and signed `SHA256SUMS`.
cards.mechanism: Build jobs copy the knowledge verification files into per-platform package artifacts. The release job recollects them, includes them in `SHA256SUMS`, signs the checksum file with Cosign keyless OIDC, and uploads the knowledge files as standalone GitHub Release assets.
cards.boundaries: Downstream verification relies on releases containing both the standalone knowledge verification files and the signed aggregate checksum set.
cards.invalid_conditions: This card becomes invalid if releases stop shipping knowledge verification assets, omit them from `SHA256SUMS`, or stop signing the release checksum set.

evidence.note: Build jobs collect the knowledge manifest and sha files into dist.
Package artifacts upload those knowledge files alongside CLI tarballs.
The release job recopies downloaded knowledge verification assets into dist.
SHA256SUMS includes knowledge bundle manifest and sha files.
Release checksums are signed with Cosign keyless OIDC.
GitHub release upload includes the knowledge verification files.

cards.title: knowledge.search is a read-only AI and chat surface over the embedded bundle
cards.summary: Redeven exposes the embedded knowledge bundle through a read-only `knowledge.search` builtin tool, and Env App renders structured knowledge-search results inside the chat tool UI.
cards.mechanism: Runtime knowledge code loads the embedded bundle once, scores cards in memory, builtin tool registration advertises `knowledge.search` as a non-mutating tool, tool execution dispatches to `knowledge.Search` behind a read-permission gate, and `ToolCallBlock` renders query, match, repo, and summary details for the result set.
cards.boundaries: The tool returns scoped summaries rather than raw file-level evidence details and does not mutate the knowledge source or dist artifacts.
cards.invalid_conditions: This card becomes invalid if knowledge queries stop reading the embedded bundle, expose raw file-level evidence by default, or bypass the read-permission gate.

evidence.note: LoadEmbeddedBundle reads the embedded bundle lazily from embed FS.
Search ranks cards in memory and caps the result count.
Builtin tool registry declares knowledge.search as a non-mutating knowledge tool.
Tool execution enforces read permission and dispatches to knowledge.Search.
Chat UI builds a dedicated knowledge display model from tool args and results.
Tool call rendering includes a dedicated Knowledge result surface in chat.

cards.title: Env App composes floe-webapp runtime and protocol with Floeterm terminal-web and Flowersec browser helpers
cards.summary: Env App is built as a Redeven-specific shell on top of floe-webapp UI and runtime primitives, floe-webapp protocol connectivity, Floeterm terminal-web components, and Flowersec browser grant helpers.
cards.mechanism: The UI package pins released versions of all four upstream packages. `EnvAppShell` imports floe-webapp providers, layout primitives, icons, and protocol hooks; terminal surfaces use Floeterm `TerminalCore` and session coordination; controlplane services call Flowersec browser helpers to exchange entry tickets for channel grants; and the upstream sibling packages define the exported interfaces these Redeven surfaces consume.
cards.boundaries: This card only holds while Env App continues to consume published upstream packages instead of local ad-hoc replacements for protocol, terminal, and grant handling.
cards.invalid_conditions: This card becomes invalid if Env App stops using these upstream package interfaces or replaces controlplane, terminal, and UI semantics with unrelated local implementations.

evidence.note: Env App pins floe-webapp-core.
Env App pins floe-webapp-protocol.
Env App pins floeterm terminal-web.
Env App pins flowersec-core.
Env App shell imports floe-webapp runtime and layout primitives.
Env App shell consumes Flowersec observer typing for runtime connections.
Env App shell consumes the floe-webapp protocol hook.
Terminal panel consumes Floeterm TerminalCore and terminal session abstractions.
Controlplane services request entry channel grants through flowersec-core/browser.
floe-webapp-core publishes app, layout, ui, and file-browser subpath exports used by Redeven.
floe-webapp protocol provider is built around Flowersec client, RPC, and reconnect primitives.
floe-webapp protocol exports grant helpers to downstream consumers.
terminal-web exports TerminalCore and session coordination APIs consumed by Env App.
terminal-web maintains UI-facing terminal session reconciliation.
Flowersec browser helpers exchange entry tickets for channel grants.
